title: Windkessel Cardiac Afterload Model
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Windkessel Cardiac Afterload Model: Windkessel model fitting: extract arterial compliance C, resistance R, and characteristic impedance Zc from pressure-flow data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.windkessel_3element…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; nonlinearity_pressure_compliance, wave_reflection_neglect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: C*dP/dt + P/R = Q - Zc*dQ/dt; 3-element Windkessel (Westkessel model)
E.observation_y: aortic pressure P(t) and flow Q(t) over one or more cardiac cycles

W.regime: Existence of the recovered windkessel_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); nonlinearity_pressure_compliance dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: pressure RMSE < 2 mmHg for calibrated 3-element WK. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |nonlinearity_pressure_compliance|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_cycles * N_params) for ODE integration per cycle per iteration
